name: Oracle Utilities Customer Cloud Service API
what_it_does: Oracle Utilities Customer Cloud Service (CCS) REST API for utility customer information and billing management. Enables AI agents to manage customer account lifecycle and service establishment for utility customer management, handle meter reading and consumption data for utility billing, access rate schedule and tariff calculation for complex utility billing automation, retrieve payment processing and collections workflow for revenue management, manage service order and field work dispatch for utility operations, handle smart meter interval data and demand response for AMI integration, access outage management and restoration workflow data for grid operations, retrieve customer engagement and self-service data for utility customer experience, manage regulatory reporting and compliance data for utility commission requirements, and integrate Oracle Utilities data with smart grid, IoT, and CRM platforms.
use_cases: Automating billing calculations from utility billing agents
Processing service orders from utility field operations agents
Managing outage response from grid operations agents
Analyzing meter data from AMI integration agents
not_for: Non-utility businesses without regulated utility billing complexity
Telecom billing without electric/gas/water utility rate structures
Small utilities without enterprise CIS requirements
best_when: A large electric, gas, or water utility using Oracle Utilities CCS wants AI agents to automate customer account management, billing, meter data processing, service orders, and regulatory compliance.
avoid_when: REGULATORY RISK: Utility billing automation must comply with state PUC/PSC tariff requirements — automated rate application errors create billing disputes and regulatory scrutiny. Demand response automation must comply with FERC and ISO/RTO demand response program rules. Smart meter data privacy regulations (California SB 1) and CPUC data rules restrict automated sharing of interval meter consumption data. Oracle Utilities rate schedule automation requires utility tariff expertise to configure correctly.

auth.notes: Oracle Utilities uses OAuth 2.0. Documentation at docs.oracle.com/en/industries/utilities. Webhooks for utility events. REST API with JSON. Austin, Texas HQ (Oracle). Public (ORCL NYSE). Oracle acquired SPL WorldGroup (2012) and Lodestar (2007) to build Oracle Utilities portfolio. Oracle CC&B (Customer Care and Billing) and MDM are legacy products; CCS is cloud replacement. 3,000+ utility customers globally. Strong in North America and international utilities. Competes with SAP IS-U and Hansen Technologies for utility CIS.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual enterprise subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription by meter count and modules
pricing.notes: Austin, Texas. Oracle (ORCL NYSE). Oracle Utilities includes CC&B, MDM, NMS, OMS. CCS is cloud migration of CC&B. 3,000+ utility customers. Strong in electric, gas, and water utilities. Competes with SAP IS-U (Energy Utilities) and Hansen for global utility CIS.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; Oracle Cloud Infrastructure limits apply

known_agent_gotchas: REGULATORY RISK: Utility tariff rate automation — automated billing rate application errors create PUC/PSC regulatory complaints and potential billing restatement obligations
Meter data privacy (California SB 1) — automated sharing or analysis of interval meter data requires compliance with state smart meter privacy regulations; data sharing consent required
CC&B to CCS migration — many utilities still on Oracle CC&B (legacy); CCS (cloud) has different API surface; verify customer deployment before integration
Complex rate calculation — time-of-use, tiered, and demand rates require tariff expertise to configure and validate; automated billing errors in complex rates can take billing cycles to detect
Outage management integration — Oracle OMS (Outage Management System) is separate from CCS; outage notification automation requires both CCS and OMS API integration
Demand response program compliance — automated demand response event dispatch must comply with FERC Order 745 and ISO/RTO program rules; non-compliant dispatch creates financial penalties
agent_readiness.security_notes: Utility CIS. SOC2, ISO27001, FedRAMP. OAuth2. US/EU/APAC. Customer, meter, and billing data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Utility CIS. SOC2, ISO27001. OAuth2. US/EU. Utility customer and meter data.
